introSection: Wildcat is a steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf currently located at Jolly Roger Amusement Park in Ocean City, Maryland, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
It has been located at three different parks thus far.

For 1979, the coaster was replaced with a new version of the same product.

After closing at Jolly Roger Amusement Park in 2001, it was put in storage before being reassembled and reopened at the same park in 2015.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This ride is one of many Schwarzkopf Wildcat models produced. The ride features a small Tire propelled lift on its way to the Chain lift hill.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Red track and yellow supports. It used to feature red track and black supports.

fullText:
Wildcat is a steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf currently located at Jolly Roger Amusement Park in Ocean City, Maryland, USA.

It has been located at three different parks thus far.

For 1979, the coaster was replaced with a new version of the same product.

After closing at Jolly Roger Amusement Park in 2001, it was put in storage before being reassembled and reopened at the same park in 2015.

This ride is one of many Schwarzkopf Wildcat models produced. The ride features a small Tire propelled lift on its way to the Chain lift hill.

Red track and yellow supports. It used to feature red track and black supports.
